exemplar.title: How does Ava DuVernay use rhetorical strategies (logos, pathos, ethos) in 13th to challenge societal perceptions of systemic racism and mass incarceration in the United States?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Ava DuVernay use rhetorical strategies (logos, pathos, ethos) in 13th to challenge societal perceptions of systemic racism and mass incarceration in the United States?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Effective integration of policy analysis and corporate influence underpins rhetorical points.
Strong contextualization of historical context and contemporary implications.
Varied and generally well-embedded references (quotations, statistics, archival images).
weaknesses: Only one formal bibliography entry and no in-text citation details.
Minor omissions (e.g., documentary release year) limit full academic precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates excellent knowledge and sustained interpretation of the documentaryâs themes and systemic drivers of incarceration. References, while well-chosen and embedded, suffer from minor citation format issues that prevent a perfect Band 5 descriptor; however, overall the studentâs grasp of context and purpose is persuasive and sustained, justifying the top mark in this criterion.

strengths: Consistent linking of logos, ethos, and pathos to audience perception.
Strong use of statistical evidence establishes a logical foundation.
Generally appropriate analysis and evaluation of rhetorical choices.
weaknesses: Analysis often remains at paraphrase rather than probing cinematic techniques (editing, sound).
Limited commentary on how sequencing and audio design deepen persuasive impact.
Conclusion lacks acknowledgment of counterarguments for a fuller critical evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student moves beyond description to offer appropriate analysis and evaluation of the documentaryâs rhetorical strategies, aligning with Band 4. Although the evaluation is clear and competently structured, it falls short of the consistent insight and originality required for Band 5, particularly in its limited treatment of formal cinematic devices and absence of critical counterargument engagement.

strengths: Effectively organized with a clear introduction, body sections by rhetorical mode, and a summative conclusion.
Smooth transitions and consistent topic sentences sustain a coherent line of inquiry.
Supporting examples (time-stamps, statistics) are well integrated within paragraphs.
weaknesses: Occasional repetition and abrupt paragraph endings hinder full cohesion.
Introduction could preview the essayâs structure more explicitly.
Some block quotations lack syntactic integration.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay is cohesively structured around a sustained line of inquiry, with evidence generally well integrated and transitions that guide the reader effectively. Minor lapses in previewing structure and quotation integration do not detract from an otherwise expertly developed and organized argument, meriting the highest mark in this criterion.

strengths: Consistently formal, academic register appropriate to an HL essay.
Clear vocabulary and largely accurate sentence construction.
Errors (tense shifts, missing commas) do not impede comprehension.
weaknesses: Sporadic informal phrasing that slightly weakens academic tone.
Punctuation inconsistencies (brackets, quotation marks around terms).
Occasional wordiness and repetition reduce precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Language throughout is clear, precise, and largely accurate, aligning with Band 4 descriptors. While the register remains appropriate and sentence-level accuracy is good, sporadic lapses in punctuation and occasional informal phrasing prevent the precision and consistency expected for a Band 5 rating.

strandSelections.reasoning: Examples (time-stamped quotations, statistics, historical facts) are generally woven into sentences rather than left to stand alone. Signal phrases introduce most evidence, and the commentary that follows usually links back to the claim. Minor lapsesâe.g., lengthy block quotations without syntactic integrationâkeep the performance from full Band 5 integration.

The student demonstrates a solid grasp of both the documentaryâs historical context (e.g., the 13th-Amendment âloopholeâ) and its contemporary implications. Discussion of expert testimony, corporate influence (ALEC/CCA) and specific policy outcomes shows sustained interpretation of how DuVernayâs text challenges prevailing perceptions of race and incarceration. Although persuasive, the treatment remains largely thematic and rhetorical rather than probing the documentaryâs formal construction in depth, so it fits the âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ descriptor.

The piece follows a clear logical structureâintroduction with thesis, three body sections organised by rhetorical mode, and a summative conclusion. Topic sentences guide the reader, transitions are smooth, and the central line of inquiry (how rhetoric challenges perceptions) is sustained. Occasional repetition and some abrupt paragraph endings prevent the argument from being âwell-developedâ enough for Band 5.

Quotations are drawn from named speakers, on-screen captions and precise time-stamps; these examples directly underpin each rhetorical point. References are varied (statistics, interviews, archival images) and normally embedded within the prose. Minor citation weaknessesâonly one formal entry in the Works Cited and no in-text bibliographic detailâprevent the reference use from reaching the âwell-chosen and effectively supportâ threshold of Band 5.

The student maintains an appropriately formal, analytical register throughout, avoiding colloquialisms and first-person comments. Occasional informal phrasing (âthe documentary was generally perceived asâ¦â) appears, yet tone remains consistently academic and suited to an HL Essay, matching Band 4âs descriptor of consistent appropriateness.

Language is clear, vocabulary suitably academic, and sentence construction largely accurate. There are sporadic tense shifts and missing commas, but they do not impede comprehension. Precision is good rather than exceptional, aligning with Band 4âs âclear with good accuracy.â

The essay moves beyond description by linking logos, ethos and pathos to viewersâ perceptions and to specific documentary moments. However, analysis often stops at paraphrase (e.g., presenting statistics or quotes) with limited commentary on cinematic techniques such as editing, sound layering, or montage that create those effects. Evaluation of authorial choices is present but generally predictable, aligning with âgenerally appropriateâ rather than âinsightful and convincing.â

annotations.comment: A comma is needed after the introductory adverb âAdditionallyâ to improve sentence clarity.
Strong use of statistical evidence in the logos section immediately establishes a logical foundation for your argument.
The brackets around â[Barack Obama]â are distracting; integrate the speakerâs name more smoothly into the text.
Develop your analysis of logos further by discussing how editing and sequencing of statistics contribute to persuasive impact.
Effective integration of policy analysis and corporate influence demonstrates solid understanding of systemic drivers of incarceration.
Consider citing specific corporate documents or ALEC legislative drafts to deepen your logos-based critique.
Avoid lengthy descriptive passages by tightening languageâsome sentences repeat previously established points.
The conclusion effectively reiterates the central argument and underscores the documentaryâs societal impact.
Deepen the concluding reflection by discussing potential limitations of the documentaryâs approach or areas for future inquiry.
The phrase âforces them to question the systemsâ could be revised for precision and stronger academic tone.
The single entry in the bibliography does not reflect the range of sources cited; expand and format your works cited according to a recognized style.
The research question is concise, focused on rhetorical strategies, and sets a clear analytical direction for the essay.
Consider adding the documentaryâs release year on the title block to contextualize the film historically and anchor your analysis more precisely.
Strong contextualization of the documentaryâs themes and methods in the introduction establishes relevance and shows good understanding of the filmâs purpose.
The phrase âthe documentary was generally perceived asâ¦â is somewhat informal; consider rephrasing to maintain an academic register (e.g., âCritics widely received the documentary asâ¦â).
The ethos section effectively catalogs expert testimony but would benefit from deeper analysis of how each sourceâs credentials shape audience perception.
Clear transition into the pathos discussion, with an explicit signpost indicating a shift in rhetorical focus.
Consider analyzing specific audio or musical elements used to heighten emotional impact, rather than focusing solely on visual juxtaposition.
Numerous in-text references to experts and statistics are not mirrored in the single bibliography entry; ensure consistency between citations and the works cited.
The introduction could preview the essayâs structure (e.g., âThis analysis will first examine ethos, then pathos, and finally logosâ) to guide the reader.
In the pathos section, consider linking how emotional appeals reinforce the ethos and logos arguments to create a more integrated evaluation.
A clearer topic sentence at the start of the logos section would enhance coherence and signal the shift from pathos to logos.
When describing ALEC and CCA, quoting directly from a corporate press release or policy document would strengthen the authenticity of your analysis.
Punctuation around the term ââcriminalsââ is inconsistent; ensure quotations and commas conform to style guidelines.
The conclusion would benefit from acknowledging potential counterarguments or limitations of DuVernayâs perspective to demonstrate critical evaluation.
